common.name: Name
common.title: Title

passwordRequirements.nonLetter: At least one number or symbol

common.passwordPolicy: Use at least 8 characters, one uppercase letter, and one number or symbol

account.title: Account
account.membershipsSubtitle: Workspaces you can access and your role in each.
account.membershipsEmpty: You are not a member of any workspace yet.
account.membershipsLoadFailed: Could not load your workspace roles.
account.membershipsHelp: Roles are assigned by a system administrator. Contact an admin if you need access changed.
account.membershipsSystemAdminNote: You are also a system administrator, which grants platform-wide admin tools beyond individual workspace roles.
account.identitySubtitle: How you authenticate to the hub.
account.identityHasPassword: A local password is set for this account.
account.identityNoPassword: No local password — sign-in is via an identity provider.
account.passwordSubtitle: Update your local password. Other sessions will be signed out.
account.passwordBlurb: Enter your current password, then choose a new one that meets the policy.
account.passwordSameAsCurrent: New password must be different from the current password.
account.passwordUnavailable: This account does not use a local password, so it cannot be changed here.
account.notificationsSubtitle: Choose which optional product emails you want to receive.
account.notificationsBlurb: These settings apply to optional alerts. Security and account-lifecycle emails are always sent.
account.notify_passwordChanged_hint: Email when your password is updated while signed in.
account.notify_aiConnectionPending_hint: Email when an agent requests access with your pairing code.
account.notify_aiConnectionApproved_hint: Email when an administrator approves an AI connection for you.
account.notify_aiConnectionRejected_hint: Email when an administrator rejects an AI connection for you.
account.notificationsAlwaysBlurb: These cannot be turned off because they are required for security or account access.

profile.title: Your profile
profile.subtitle: Manage how your name appears in the hub. Email is your sign-in identity.
profile.emailHint: Email cannot be changed here. Ask an administrator if you need a different address.
profile.avatarHint: Optional photo (JPEG, PNG, or WebP, max 1 MB). Without a photo, a monogram is shown.
profile.closeBlurb: Permanently close your account. You will be signed out and cannot sign in again with this email until an administrator invites you anew.
profile.closeWarning1: Closing your account disables sign-in, revokes sessions, and frees your email. Authored knowledge records remain in the hub.
profile.closeWarning2: This cannot be undone from your side. Type CLOSE below and confirm that you want to close the account.
profile.closeAcknowledge: I understand that my account will be closed and I will be signed out immediately.

aiConnections.title: AI connections
aiConnections.subtitle: Set up Cursor, ChatGPT, Claude, and other clients with a guided wizard, or pair an agent that requests access.
aiConnections.wizardBlurb: Create an API client for one of your workspaces, test the connection, then copy the config for your AI tool.
aiConnections.wizardPreflightBlurb: Checks that the hub API is healthy and shows the public MCP URL your client will use.
aiConnections.wizardConfigureBlurb: Choose your AI tool, access mode, and a workspace you belong to. Write mode acts as you.
aiConnections.wizardWriteHint: Write access creates and updates draft knowledge records as you. At least one workspace is required.
aiConnections.pairingSectionBlurb: Optional: for agents that implement Knowledge Hub autodiscover. Prefer the setup wizard above for Cursor, ChatGPT, Claude, Copilot, and OpenWebUI.
aiConnections.pairingBlurb: Create a code, paste it into your AI chat with the discover URL, and the agent can request a connection.
aiConnections.tokenWizardHint: The wizard above can also show client-specific setup schemas once a token is issued.
aiConnections.pasteHint: Tell the AI to open {url} and use this pairing code.
aiConnections.approveHint: Choose scopes and optional workspace allowlist. Write access requires at least one workspace.
aiConnections.writeNeedsWorkspace: knowledge:write requires at least one workspace.
aiConnections.tokenOnce: Copy this token now if the AI has not claimed it yet.
aiConnections.tokenOnceHint: The AI can also claim this token once via the discover poll endpoint.

aiDiscover.title: AI autodiscover
aiDiscover.subtitle: Instructions for agents that want to connect to Project Knowledge Hub via MCP or OpenAPI.
aiDiscover.forAgentsBlurb: Fetch the machine-readable document first, then request an API client with a user pairing code.
aiDiscover.step1: User creates an active account and signs in.
aiDiscover.step2: User opens Profile → Connect AI and generates a pairing code.
aiDiscover.step3: User pastes the pairing code and this page URL into the AI chat.
aiDiscover.step4: AI POSTs /api/v1/ai-discover/requests with the pairing code.
aiDiscover.step5: User or a system admin approves the pending request.
aiDiscover.step6: AI polls the claim endpoint, saves the bearer token, and uses /mcp or OpenAPI tools.
aiDiscover.requestBlurb: Public endpoint (rate limited). Pairing code is required.
aiDiscover.pollBlurb: Poll until status is active. The bearer token is returned once.
aiDiscover.forHumansBlurb: Register or sign in, then manage pairing codes under Connect AI.

archive.confirmArchive: Archive “{name}”? It will disappear from normal lists and search until restored.
archive.archiveHint: This is a soft archive (not a permanent delete). You can restore it later from Archived items or Admin → Archive.
archive.deleteHintProject: This cannot be undone. Linked systems, knowledge records, and git connections stay in the workspace but are unlinked from this project.
archive.deleteHintWorkspace: This cannot be undone. All projects, systems, knowledge records, git connections, and memberships in this workspace will be permanently deleted.
archive.deleteHintSystem: This cannot be undone. Linked knowledge records and imports stay in the workspace but are unlinked from this system.
archive.deleteHintRecord: This cannot be undone. Version history and tags for this record are removed permanently.
archive.deleteHintRecordGitManaged: This record is git-managed and may reappear on the next repository sync.
archive.deleteHintImport: This cannot be undone. Draft knowledge records created from this import are kept; only the import paste and its draft links are removed.
archive.deleteAcknowledge: I understand this cannot be undone
archive.userBlurb: Soft-archived items in workspaces you can access. Restore anything you still need.
archive.workspaceBlurb: Soft-archived projects, systems, and knowledge records in this workspace.
archive.adminBlurb: Review and restore soft-archived workspaces, projects, systems, and knowledge records across the platform.

login.subtitle: Sign in with a local administrator account.
login.passwordSet: Password updated. You can sign in now.

register.title: Registration
register.subtitle: Create an account. You will confirm by email, then an administrator grants access.
register.tooShort: Password must be at least 8 characters
register.weakPassword: Use at least 8 characters, one uppercase letter, and one number or symbol
register.checkEmail: Check your email to confirm your address.
register.checkEmailHint: After you confirm, an administrator must approve your access before you can sign in.
register.resendSuccess: If that email is awaiting confirmation, a new link was sent.

confirmEmail.title: Confirm email
confirmEmail.subtitle: Verify your email address to continue registration.
confirmEmail.successHint: An administrator must approve your access before you can sign in.
confirmEmail.missingToken: This link is missing a token.
confirmEmail.expiredLink: This link has expired. Request a new confirmation from registration.
confirmEmail.usedLink: This link has already been used.

forgotPassword.title: Forgot password
forgotPassword.subtitle: Enter your email and we will send a reset link if an account exists.
forgotPassword.success: If an account exists for that email, password reset instructions were sent.

setPassword.title: Set password
setPassword.subtitle: Choose a new password for your account.
setPassword.tooShort: Password must be at least 8 characters
setPassword.missingToken: This link is missing a token.
setPassword.expiredLink: This link has expired. Request a new one.
setPassword.usedLink: This link has already been used.

dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.browseBlurb: Open the full list of workspaces you can access.
dashboard.emptyWorkspaces: You do not have any workspaces yet.
dashboard.moreWorkspaces: +{count} more — open View all to see every workspace.
dashboard.searchBlurb: Find records across a workspace by title, tags, and content.
dashboard.adminBlurb: Organizations, users, API clients, and platform setup.
dashboard.recentEmpty: No recent projects, systems, or records yet.

workspaces.title: Workspaces
workspaces.emptyFiltered: No workspaces match this search or filter.
workspaces.sectionEmptyFiltered: No items match this search or filter.
workspaces.manageImportsHint: Paste chats or (soon) convert documents and images into draft knowledge
workspaces.manageDescription: Details, sync, archive, and appearance settings for this workspace.
workspaces.manageDetailsHint: Description, ID, owners, dates, and counts
workspaces.descriptionPlaceholder: Short overview shown on the workspace page
workspaces.descriptionTooLong: Description must be at most {max} characters
workspaces.detailsStatRecords: {count, plural, one {# knowledge record} other {# knowledge records}}
workspaces.detailsStatGit: {count, plural, one {# Git connection} other {# Git connections}}
workspaces.detailsStatAdmins: {count, plural, one {# workspace admin} other {# workspace admins}}
workspaces.manageArchivedItemsHint: View and restore soft-archived projects, systems, and records
workspaces.manageColorHint: Choose a tile accent color for this workspace
workspaces.manageArchiveHint: Soft-archive this workspace. You can restore it later.
workspaces.manageRestoreHint: Bring this workspace back to active use.
workspaces.manageDeleteHint: Permanently remove this workspace and everything inside it. This cannot be undone.
workspaces.statusNeedsAttentionHint: Open the area that needs attention
workspaces.colorHint: Pick an accent so this workspace is easier to spot in lists and on the dashboard. Auto chooses a stable color from the workspace id.

projects.noLinkedSystems: No systems linked to this project.
projects.noLinkedKnowledge: No knowledge records linked to this project.
projects.manageDescription: Details, edit, archive, or permanently delete this project.
projects.manageDetailsHint: ID, slug, status, tags, and timestamps
projects.manageEditHint: Update name, summary, description, status, and tags
projects.manageArchiveHint: Soft-archive this project. You can restore it later.
projects.manageRestoreHint: Bring this project back to active use.
projects.manageDeleteHint: Permanently remove this project. Linked items stay but are unlinked.

systems.manageDescription: Details, edit, archive, or permanently delete this system.
systems.manageDetailsHint: ID, slug, type, environment, tags, and timestamps
systems.manageEditHint: Update name, project link, summary, status, and tags
systems.manageArchiveHint: Soft-archive this system. You can restore it later.
systems.manageRestoreHint: Bring this system back to active use.
systems.manageDeleteHint: Permanently remove this system. Linked records stay but are unlinked.

records.manageDescription: Details, edit, history, archive, or permanently delete this record.
records.manageDetailsHint: Summary, project, system, source, and verification
records.manageArchiveHint: Soft-archive this record. You can restore it later.
records.manageRestoreHint: Bring this record back to active use.
records.manageDeleteHint: Permanently remove this record and its version history.
records.historicalWarningList: Historical version — read-only. Restore creates a new version.
records.historicalWarningDetail: You are viewing a historical version. It is read-only; restoring creates a new version.
records.supersededWarning: This record has been superseded by a newer current configuration in the same series.
records.gitManagedReadOnly: This record is git-managed. Edit the file in the repository and run Sync — hub edits are blocked.

search.title: Search
search.subtitle: Full-text search across knowledge records in a workspace.

admin.title: Admin
admin.overviewBlurb: Platform administration for users, access, API clients, and audit history.
admin.overviewHiddenHint: Hidden setup cards stay available in the admin menu. Check a box to show a card here again.
admin.overviewShowMcp: Show LLM / MCP setup on overview
admin.mailOverviewBlurb: SMTP or Resend for invites and password reset. Overrides .env when saved here.
admin.mailSettingsPageBlurb: Configure outbound email for invites and forgotten-password messages.
admin.mailSettingsBlurb: Saved settings override MAIL_* environment variables. SMTP password and API keys are stored in the database (not shown again); leave those fields blank to keep the current value.
admin.mailResendHint: Create an API key in Resend (or a FreeResend-compatible endpoint later). From address must be a verified domain/sender.
admin.mailConsoleHint: Messages are written to the API process log. Use this for local development.
admin.mailSecretLeaveBlank: Leave blank to keep existing secret
admin.mailTestBlurb: Sends a short message with the currently effective driver (after save).
admin.mcpWizardBlurb: Guided steps to create an API client, verify the connection, and copy schemas for Cursor, ChatGPT, Claude, Antigravity, Gemini API, Copilot, and OpenWebUI.
admin.mcpWizardPreflightBlurb: Confirm the API is healthy and ready before creating an LLM client.
admin.mcpWizardPublicUrlHint: Use when Cursor must reach MCP on a different host than WEB_URL. Leave empty to use MCP_PUBLIC_URL or {WEB_URL}/mcp. Host-only values get /mcp appended. Internal API_URL (e.g. http://api:3101) is only for server-side Docker networking.
admin.mcpWizardDoneBlurb: Your API client is ready. Paste the config into your AI tool, then try a search prompt against your workspace.
admin.mcpWizardDoneBlurbNamed: “{name}” is ready. Paste the config into your AI tool, then try a search prompt against your workspace.
admin.mcpWizardDoneNextUser: The one-time token was shown earlier — if you lost it, rotate the client from Your connections below.
admin.mcpWizardDoneNextAdmin: The one-time token was shown earlier — rotate the client under Admin → API clients if it was lost.
admin.mcpWizardTroubleshootIntro: If the AI tool cannot reach Knowledge Hub after you paste the config, try these checks.
admin.mcpWizardTrouble_pasteToken: Confirm the full token was pasted (starts with kh_) and that Authorization uses Bearer auth.
admin.mcpWizardTrouble_reloadClient: Reload or restart the AI client after saving MCP settings (Cursor: Settings → MCP → refresh).
admin.mcpWizardTrouble_httpsReachable: Cloud clients (ChatGPT, Claude.ai, Copilot) need a public HTTPS MCP URL — localhost will not work for them.
admin.mcpWizardTrouble_workspaceScope: Search only works in workspaces allowed on the client; pick the same workspace you configured here.
admin.mcpWizardTrouble_rotateToken: If the token was lost or leaked, rotate it from AI connections (or Admin → API clients) and update the client config.
admin.mcpWizardTroubleAdmin_urlSource: Check the public MCP URL source (override / MCP_PUBLIC_URL / WEB_URL). Clients must use the public URL, not the internal API_URL Docker hostname.
admin.mcpWizardTroubleAdmin_middleware: Web middleware must allow /mcp through to the API with Bearer auth (not redirect to login). initialize EOF often means HTML login was returned.
admin.mcpWizardTroubleAdmin_wellKnown: /.well-known/* must return JSON 404, not the login HTML page — otherwise OAuth discovery proxies crash on <!DOCTYPE.
admin.mcpWizardTroubleAdmin_curl: Probe MCP with curl: POST initialize to {mcpUrl} with Authorization: Bearer <token> and Accept/Content-Type application/json. Expect MCP JSON, not HTML or 401.
admin.mcpWizardTroubleAdmin_docker: Inside Compose, API_URL may be http://api:3101 while browsers/LLMs need WEB_URL or MCP_PUBLIC_URL on the published host.
admin.mcpWizardTroubleAdmin_rateLimit: Repeated failed tests can hit rate limits — wait a minute, then re-run connection tests from this wizard.
admin.mcpWizardConfigureBlurb: Choose the target LLM platform, access mode, and workspace. The matching schema is generated at the end.
admin.mcpWizardTargetClientHint: Select which platform you will connect. This chooses the config format shown in the last step.
admin.mcpWizardActingUserRequired: Draft write requires an acting user.
admin.mcpWizardMissingFields: Name, organization, and workspace are required.
admin.mcpWizardClientCreated: Client “{name}” is ready. Copy the token now.
admin.mcpWizardTestBlurb: Initialize MCP, list tools, and optionally call search_knowledge.
admin.mcpWizardClientBlurb_cursor: Paste into Cursor MCP settings or `.cursor/mcp.json`, then reload MCP servers.
admin.mcpWizardClientBlurb_chatgpt: Create a Custom GPT Action: import the OpenAPI schema, then set authentication to API Key (Bearer) with your token.
admin.mcpWizardClientBlurb_claude: Use MCP for Claude Desktop, Claude Code, and claude.ai chat (custom remote connector). Not OpenAPI Actions.
admin.mcpWizardClientBlurb_antigravity: Consumer Google AI (Pro/free): use Antigravity CLI (`agy`) with the Bearer stdio proxy. Gemini CLI is enterprise-only since June 2026.
admin.mcpWizardClientBlurb_gemini: Gemini API / Vertex: OpenAPI tools or functionDeclarations. Enterprise Gemini CLI can use the MCP JSON; individuals should pick Antigravity instead.
admin.mcpWizardClientBlurb_copilot: Import the Swagger 2.0 file as a Copilot Studio / Power Platform custom connector (MCP streamable).
admin.mcpWizardClientBlurb_openwebui: Prefer native MCP (Streamable HTTP), or add an OpenAPI external tool pointing at the published schema URL.
admin.mcpWizardClientHint1_cursor: Keep the bearer token secret; treat it like a password.
admin.mcpWizardClientHint2_cursor: After pasting, open Cursor Settings → MCP and confirm the server is connected.
admin.mcpWizardClientHint3_cursor: Try a prompt that uses search_knowledge against your workspace.
admin.mcpWizardClientHint1_chatgpt: The API host must be reachable from OpenAI (public HTTPS URL).
admin.mcpWizardClientHint2_chatgpt: In Actions auth, choose API Key / Bearer and paste the issued token.
admin.mcpWizardClientHint3_chatgpt: You can also Import from URL using the openApiUrl in the auth snippet.
admin.mcpWizardClientHint1_claude: Desktop/Code: paste the MCP JSON (same shape as Cursor) with Bearer auth.
admin.mcpWizardClientHint2_claude: claude.ai: Settings → Connectors → add a custom remote MCP URL; auth is Bearer header.
admin.mcpWizardClientHint3_claude: Remote chat connectors call your public /mcp from Anthropic’s cloud — HTTPS required.
admin.mcpWizardClientHint1_antigravity: Install with: irm https://antigravity.google/cli/install.ps1 | iex (Windows) or the install script from antigravity.google (macOS/Linux).
admin.mcpWizardClientHint2_antigravity: Download scripts/mcp-bearer-stdio-proxy.mjs, then save the config as %USERPROFILE%\.gemini\config\mcp_config.json (fix the proxy path).
admin.mcpWizardClientHint3_antigravity: Quit agy fully, restart, run /mcp — avoid mcp-remote and raw serverUrl headers (they fail on this hub).
admin.mcpWizardClientHint1_gemini: For Google AI Pro/free terminal use, switch to the Antigravity client tab instead of Gemini CLI.
admin.mcpWizardClientHint2_gemini: functionDeclarations are sanitized for Gemini’s OpenAPI subset (API / Vertex).
admin.mcpWizardClientHint3_gemini: OpenAPI clients should call the same `/api/v1/llm/tools/*` endpoints with Bearer auth.
admin.mcpWizardClientHint1_copilot: Use x-ms-agentic-protocol mcp-streamable-1.0 (included in the schema).
admin.mcpWizardClientHint2_copilot: Set connector auth to API key on header Authorization with value Bearer <token>.
admin.mcpWizardClientHint3_copilot: Host/port come from your public MCP URL; update the override if needed.
admin.mcpWizardClientHint1_openwebui: Admin → Settings → Integrations → Manage Tool Servers → Add Connection; set Connection Type to MCP (Streamable HTTP).
admin.mcpWizardClientHint2_openwebui: Grant Access Control (e.g. public read); empty grants hide the server. Enable it in chat via + → Integrations → Tools (not model Features).
admin.mcpWizardClientHint3_openwebui: OpenAPI fallback: `/api/v1/llm/openapi.json` + Bearer. Prefer a tool-capable model; tiny local models often skip tools.
admin.organizationsBlurb: Create and edit organizations (name and slug). Slugs must be unique.
admin.organizationDeleteConfirm: Delete “{name}”? Choose whether to transfer inherited items, or permanently delete them with the organization.
admin.organizationDeleteLastHint: Cannot delete the last remaining organization.
admin.organizationDeleteDisposition: What should happen to inherited items?
admin.organizationDeleteModeTransfer: Transfer workspaces, tags, and API clients to another organization (recommended)
admin.organizationDeleteModeDestroy: Permanently delete all inherited items — do not transfer
admin.organizationDestroyWarning1: Warning 1 of 2: Deleting “{name}” this way permanently removes its workspaces ({count}), tags, and API clients. This cannot be undone.
admin.organizationDestroyWarning2: Warning 2 of 2: Confirm you want to wipe every inherited item for “{name}”. Nothing will be moved to another organization.
admin.organizationDestroyAcknowledge: I understand that workspaces, tags, and API clients will be permanently deleted and cannot be recovered.
admin.organizationDestroyConfirmFinal: Delete organization and all inherited items
admin.organizationDestroyDoubleConfirmRequired: Acknowledge both destroy warnings before permanently deleting inherited items.
admin.organizationTransferHint: Choose which organization should receive workspaces, tags, and API clients.
admin.organizationTransferAutoHint: Only one organization remains — items will transfer to “{target}”.
admin.organizationTransferRequired: Select an organization to receive inherited items before deleting.
admin.organizationWorkspaceCount: {count, plural, =0 {No workspaces} one {# workspace} other {# workspaces}}
admin.slugOptionalHint: Optional — derived from the name if left empty
admin.organizationSlugHint: Slug is used in URLs and integrations. Changing it does not move existing workspaces.
admin.auditBlurb: Search, filter by day or date range, page through platform audit events, and export the current filtered view.
admin.auditExportHint: Exports use the current filters (not only this page). Each export is itself recorded in the audit log. PDF pages include organization, project, filters, and export timestamp in the header and footer.
admin.auditExportTooLarge: Too many matching events ({count}). Narrow filters to at most {max} before exporting.
admin.auditResultCount: {count, plural, =0 {No events} one {# event} other {# events}}
admin.removeUserWarning1: Remove “{name}”? They will lose sign-in access. Authored knowledge records stay in the hub.
admin.removeUserWarning2: Final confirmation: “{name}” will be closed (sessions revoked, credentials cleared). This cannot be undone from the admin UI alone.
admin.removeUserAcknowledge: I understand that this account will be closed and the user cannot sign in again.
admin.purgeUserOption: Hard delete this user and their authored knowledge (development only)
admin.purgeUserWarning1: Permanently purge “{name}”? This deletes the user row and knowledge they authored. Dev/test only.
admin.purgeUserWarning2: Final confirmation: “{name}” and their authored records / git connections will be deleted from the database.
admin.purgeUserAcknowledge: I understand this is a permanent database purge for development cleanup.
admin.purgeClosedHint: This account is already closed. You can purge it from the database in development.
admin.emptyUsersFiltered: No users match this search or filter.
admin.usersPageBlurb: Invite users, review signup requests, and manage accounts.
admin.pendingApprovalHint: These users confirmed their email. Approve with at least one workspace role, or reject.
admin.pendingEmailHint: Signed up but have not confirmed their email yet.
admin.approveHint: Assign at least one workspace membership. The user can sign in after approval.
admin.approveNeedsMembership: Add at least one workspace membership.
admin.approveDuplicateWorkspace: Each workspace may only appear once.
admin.approveNoWorkspaces: Create a workspace before approving users.
admin.rejectConfirm: Reject signup for {email}? The account will be disabled.
admin.inviteHint: The user receives an email to set their password and activate the account.
admin.membershipNoWorkspacesLeft: This user is already a member of every workspace.
admin.idpStubHint: Stub for future SSO (leave empty for local)
admin.passwordHint: At least 8 characters, one uppercase letter, and one number or symbol
admin.tokenOnce: Copy this token now. It will not be shown again.
admin.emptyClientsFiltered: No API clients match this search or filter.
admin.emptyActiveClientsFiltered: No active API clients match this search or filter.
admin.emptyMembershipsFiltered: No memberships match this search or filter.
admin.toastOrganizationDeletedTransferred: Organization “{name}” deleted. Inherited items moved to “{target}”.
admin.toastOrganizationDeletedDestroyed: Organization “{name}” deleted. All inherited items were permanently removed.
admin.toastApiClientCreated: API client “{name}” created. Copy the token now.
admin.toastApiClientRotated: API client token rotated. Copy the new token now.
admin.pendingApiClientsHint: Agents requested access with a user pairing code. Approve or reject like signup requests.
admin.approveApiClientHint: Requested by {user}. Adjust scopes and workspace allowlist before issuing a token.
admin.writeScopeHint: knowledge:write requires an acting user and at least one workspace allowlist entry.
admin.monitoringBlurb: Platform health, MCP activity, sessions, and database backup export/import.
admin.monitoringMcpEmpty: No MCP audit events in this range.
admin.monitoringClientsEmpty: No MCP client traffic in this range.
admin.monitoringMaintenanceBlurb: Embedding reindex and soft-archive visibility. Hard purge remains per-entity from Archive.
admin.monitoringPurgeHint: Review soft-archived entities and purge individually in
admin.monitoringBackupsBlurb: Scheduled dumps land in BACKUP_DIR. Export creates a fresh dump; import fully replaces the database (type REPLACE).
admin.monitoringImportWarning: This replaces the entire database while services are running. Sessions on Postgres are terminated first; the API restarts afterward — also restart worker in Dokploy. Log in with users from the imported dump (not the old Dokploy seed). Secrets stay in env.
admin.monitoringImportNeedArtifact: Choose an artifact or upload a dump file.
admin.monitoringRetentionBlurb: Keep storage low: retain recent dailies, then sparse weekly/monthly dumps. Saved to BACKUP_DIR/retention.json (also used by the backup sidecar).
admin.monitoringAutoRotate: Auto-rotate after each export / scheduled dump
admin.storageSettingsPageBlurb: Configure external object storage for offsite database backups (S3-compatible today). Azure Blob will appear here with Microsoft Entra ID sign-in. Env remains the fallback when no override is saved.
admin.storageSettingsBlurb: Saved settings override BLOB_* environment variables. Access keys are stored encrypted-at-rest in the database; leave blank to keep the current value.
admin.storageDisabledHint: With storage disabled, Monitoring export stays on the local backup volume only.
admin.storageSecretLeaveBlank: Leave blank to keep existing secret

imports.title: Imports
imports.subtitle: Bring chats, documents, and media into {workspace} as draft knowledge.
imports.pickTypeBlurb: Start with a paste today. Document and image conversion will land in later steps.
imports.typeHint_paste_chat: Paste an LLM conversation or Markdown, then create draft knowledge records.
imports.typeHint_documents: Upload DOCX, PDF, or Excel and convert to Markdown with a record type.
imports.typeHint_images: Upload pictures and attach a Markdown description so agents can find them.
imports.createHelp: Raw paste is stored separately and never appears in MCP or search. Create draft records from the import next.
imports.detailSubtitle: Review the raw paste and create one or more draft knowledge records.
imports.rawContentHelp: Raw import body (not searchable via MCP).
imports.createDraftHelp: Edit the excerpt below to convert only part of the paste, then save as a draft knowledge record.
imports.excerptNotePlaceholder: What part of the conversation is this?
imports.manageDescription: Details, archive, or permanently delete this conversation import.
imports.manageDetailsHint: Format, source, model, and linked drafts
imports.manageArchiveHint: Hide this import from the default list. Drafts created from it are kept.
imports.manageDeleteHint: Permanently remove this paste. Draft knowledge records are kept.

gitSync.title: Synchronizations
gitSync.subtitle: Pull Markdown documentation into {workspace} from connected repositories.
gitSync.addBlurb: Choose a Git host. All listed providers sync Markdown via a personal access token (PAT).
gitSync.connectBlurb_github: Uses a personal access token with contents:read. Default path mappings categorize docs/adr, deployment, product, and more.
gitSync.connectBlurb_gitlab: Uses a GitLab personal access token with read_api / read_repository. Optional base URL for self-hosted GitLab.
gitSync.connectBlurb_azure_devops: Uses a PAT with Code (Read). Owner is the organization; repository is project/repo.
gitSync.connectBlurb_bitbucket: Uses a Bitbucket app password or access token with repository read.
gitSync.connectBlurb_forgejo: Uses a Forgejo/Gitea access token. Instance base URL is required (e.g. https://git.example.com).
gitSync.baseUrlRequired: Instance base URL is required for this provider.
gitSync.webhookLeaveBlank: Leave blank to keep existing secret
gitSync.empty: No synchronizations yet. Use Add to connect a source.
gitSync.clearHistoryConfirm: Clear all finished sync history for this connection? This cannot be undone.
gitSync.safetySweepHint: A safety re-sync also runs about once every 24 hours while the worker is up (covers missed webhooks).
